iterations.patch: skills/things-cli/SKILL.md +1/-1 (compound-write hierarchy / immediate-parent / UUID-chain at the precondition entry point)

loop.outcome: 0 accepted / 2 reverted; clean 2-consecutive-no-accept stop. Both refiner attempts chased the remaining graded success failure (compound-garden-shed) rather than compressing SKILL.md (the ladder ranks success above friction/context), and both regressed validation. NO compression/repositioning patch survived, so friction and context were NOT recovered; the final v2 skill content is byte-identical to c50cc67 (PR #246 head).

confirmation.design: paired, 10 reps, validation+holdout splits only (12 tasks), 120 runs/config, serialized, world-seed 1

v2final.skill: v2 (in-tree, c50cc67 — unchanged by the loop)

validation.ties: recovery-missing-area 10/10, validation-reads-deadlines 10/10, validation-writes-set-deadline 10/10, validation-compound-trip-checklist 6/6-of-10, validation-reasoning-heading-inheritance 6/6-of-10, gui-placement-room214 1/10 (both)

confirmation.somedayRegression: writes-someday-holdout ('Move X to Someday, no date') requires `things todo update <ref> --when someday` (start=2, startDate NULL). The prompt verb 'Move' traps agents onto the `todo move` verb (which takes --area/--project/--heading). Control v0.2 surfaces `--when someday` on the write path directly, so its agents reliably reach `update --when someday` (10/10). v2 defers scheduling parameters to `things help writes` and separates the when-vocabulary from the verb list, so v2 agents more often guess unsupported move flags (--to someday, --inbox, --detach) and never reach update --when someday (5/10). Copy-attributable, not fixture flake (paired, same world-seed, control 10/10 proves satisfiable).

verdict.successGate: FAILS. Non-inferiority required on BOTH splits. Validation: v2 63/80 > control 54/80 (BETTER, +9, entirely recurrence: marquee +5, project +4). Holdout: v2 32/40 < control 38/40 (INFERIOR, -6, driven entirely by writes-someday-holdout 5/10 vs 10/10). Holdout non-inferiority is not met.
verdict.friction: v2 WORSE (1.17 vs 0.90) — unrecovered by the loop.
verdict.context: v2 WORSE — dynamic 7682 vs 6156 (+25%); always-loaded SKILL.md 6746 vs 5457 B (+24%) — unrecovered by the loop.
verdict.decision: DO NOT PROMOTE v2 as a wholesale replacement of v0.2 at 10 reps. It fails the success gate (holdout-inferior) and is worse on friction and context. The N=3 sweep's tied holdout (11/12 both) was masking variance — 10 reps exposes the someday-write regression, vindicating the >=10-rep promotion rule.
verdict.recommendationForMike: The recurrence-family win (marquee, project) is real, large, and isolatable. Options: (1) cherry-pick the recurrence contract lift into v0.2 WITHOUT the full demarcation (keep v0.2 someday/writes copy) and re-confirm; (2) fix the v2 someday-write regression (restore explicit 'to schedule/park use update --when someday; move takes --area/--project/--heading only' on the write path) and re-confirm holdout; (3) keep v0.2. A CLI-side hint on `todo move` for scheduling intents (--to someday etc.) is a separate oddity candidate.
